topic: A close-up of a 'Vinyl Record Player' being started.

parsed_generation.script:
Scene 1 — Power On (0-2s): Macro close-up: power switch ON; LED glow; platter begins to spin.
Scene 2 — Tonearm Lowering (2-5s): Top-down/side view: tonearm glides toward the record; needle lowers and contacts groove.
Scene 3 — Spin and Focus (5-8s): Macro close-up: platter spins steadily; stylus tracks groove; light glints across the vinyl surface.
parsed_generation.narration: The moment the turntable wakes is defined by precise, deliberate motion. As the needle lands, analog warmth starts translating grooves into sound. This tight close-up reveals the quiet mechanics behind vinyl's living rhythm.

feedback: The sequence stays tightly on a vinyl turntable start and the narration adds context about analog warmth. Improve alignment with the reference by adopting shorter, more punchy beats and a single-action focus per shot; emphasize a decisive end to each scene (e.g., needle lands, platter spins). Add a couple of tangible visual textures (grooves, gloss from the record, LED glow) to boost visual richness without introducing new subjects.
strengths: Clear single-subject focus on the vinyl turntable and its mechanical actions.
fixes: Adopt even more concise, action-first phrasing to mimic reference style.
Explicitly end scenes with a decisive action (e.g., 'needle lands', 'platter spins') and enhance texture descriptions.
